title: Cosmia prostrata

taxonomy: Cosmia prostrata is a species of moth in the family Noctuidae. It is a member of the genus Cosmia. The family Noctuidae, also known as the owlet moths or owlet moths and allies, is a large and diverse group of moths. intro: Cosmia prostrata, also known as Creeping Valerian, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the Valerianaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as relief from anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative and calming effects. The bioactive constituents of Cosmia prostrata include valepotriates, valerenic acid, and isovaleric acid, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality.
